item.title: Australia defeats South Africa in opening clash of women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Australia puts its rivals on notice with a dominant victory over South Africa to kick-off the women's T20 World Cup.

item.title: Adelaide Thunderbirds break Super Netball record with 13-win season
item.description: This year's record-breaking Adelaide Thunderbirds are shifting their focus to the Super Netball finals, with a third premiership in four years on offer.

item.title: Former WA potato farm transformed into wetland sanctuary for rare and endangered birds
item.description: Once drained for farm irrigation, this former potato farm now attracts birds from as far away as Siberia.

item.title: Maroons rally around Tino Fa'asuamaleaui after father's death ahead of Origin II
item.description: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui indicates he plans to play in Origin II after the death of his father this week.

item.title: Brisbane City Council to invest more than $5 million in health and wellness activities in Wednesday's budget
item.description: The Move Well Brisbane campaign will double the number of providers and activities in Brisbane's Active and Healthy program and include nutritional and cooking classes.

item.title: Luai leads Tigers to epic final Leichhardt win
item.description: Jarome Luai has lead the Wests Tigers to a win in their final match at Leichhardt Oval before it's redevelopment.

item.title: This week in history quiz, June 15: Test yourself on these past events
item.description: When was Salt Lake City awarded the XIX Winter Olympics? And how much do you know about the Fast and the Furious films? Test yourself in this week's history quiz.

item.title: Gold Coast nurse charged over alleged offences involving six teenage girls
item.description: Detectives charged the 58-year-old man following investigations into the alleged indecent treatment of children between November 2020 and May 2024.

item.title: Why Cameron Munster is always Queensland's State of Origin saviour
item.description: Ever since his first State of Origin match, Queensland has looked to Cameron Munster in times of trouble. Now, with the series on the line at the MCG, his state will ask its greatest modern hero to do so again.

item.title: Cairns man charged after allegedly naming prominent Queenslander in extortion case
item.description: A Cairns man accused of exposing the identity of a Queensland public figure at the centre of a mysterious extortion case has been charged by police with allegedly disobeying a suppression order.

item.title: UK prime minister says he will ban social media for under-16s
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer says he will ban social media sites for under-16s and impose restrictions on gaming and live streaming platforms in some of the most far-reaching online restrictions globally.

item.title: Bold BBL plan within reach after Cricket Australia meets with states
item.description: A meeting between Cricket Australia and the six state bodies has ended in significant steps being taken towards BBL privatisation.

item.title: Super Netball Round-Up: As progress dips in men's netball, the Giants may have played their last game
item.description: Despite male netballers making a valuable contribution to the Aussie Diamonds pathway and Super Netball competition, progress between the two genders at the international level has largely been scaled back and unexplained.

item.title: Interest rates and tax shake-up see buyer demand weaken in property market
item.description: Interest rate hikes, tax changes and global economic uncertainty force first home buyers and investors to press pause on property purchases.

item.title: Saving might feel impossible, but there are five reasons to keep trying
item.description: Saving is not the only tool you need to achieve financial security these days, but it still has a role to play if you can manage it.

item.title: Advocate says algal bloom information 'inconsistent', amid survey of health impacts of the bloom
item.description: Volunteers surveyed more than 200 people who reported a range of symptoms during the algal bloom outbreak not represented in the SA government's health advice. A disability advocate says health information has been "inaccessible" and "inconsistent".

item.title: Eight crew presumed dead in B-52 bomber crash at US Air Force base in California
item.description: A US Air Force B-52 bomber, designed to carry nuclear and conventional weapons, crashes shortly after taking off at an air base.

item.title: Tasmanian police officer Keith Smith remembered at Ulverstone memorial service one year after he was killed
item.description: A Tasmanian police officer who was killed while on duty is remembered for his compassion and care during a memorial service, one year after his death.

item.title: Emergency care delayed before death of wellness influencer Stacey Warnecke, court hears
item.description: A court probing the death of Stacey Warnecke after a freebirth has heard that emergency care was delayed for several minutes because her "birth keeper" would only call an ambulance if she consented.

item.title: 'Global' midwife shortage temporarily shuts Crystal Brook and Jamestown birthing services in SA's Mid North
item.description: Two more South Australian regional centres are now temporarily diverting expectant mothers as Kapunda Hospital's birthing service is closed indefinitely.

item.title: Blues coach Laurie Daley reveals Ethan Strange's role in State of Origin II against Maroons at MCG
item.description: After his starring role for the Blues in State of Origin I, Ethan Srange is given a new assignment against the Maroons at the MCG on Wednesday night.

item.title: Ann Patchett rewrites the step-family narrative in her new novel, Whistler
item.description: The bestselling author of books including Bel Canto, Tom Lake and her latest, Whistler, is determined to see the good in everyone.

item.title: What time is State of Origin II kick-off? How to watch Blues vs Maroons at MCG
item.description: NSW and Queensland meet at the famous MCG in State of Origin Game II. Here is what you need to know ahead of tonight's match.

item.title: NDIS participants highlight mental toll of navigating 'complex' system
item.description: Participants say they are "exhausted" and "overwhelmed" by trying to navigate the complex and ever-changing NDIS system.

item.title: B-52 bomber crashed at more than a kilometre per minute at US Air Force base
item.description: A B-52 bomber aircraft that crashed at a US Air Force base in California, killing eight people aboard, made a sharp right and then nearly completed a 180-degree turn before plunging to the ground.

item.title: State of Origin coaches back referee Ashley Klein after gambling history made public
item.description: Billy Slater and Laurie Daley come to the defence of referee Ashley Klein after his gambling debt history was revealed earlier in the week.

item.title: More support urged for men's suicide prevention as advocates call for greater focus on situational distress
item.description: There are urgent calls for more customised support services for men and boys experiencing situational distress.

item.title: Pastoralists take to the skies to track down livestock lost in floods
item.description: Closed roads and flooded plains are making livestock inaccessible for some pastoralists, while others are employing some serious technology to track down their animals.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands's $12m settlement an 'exceptionally good deal' for ARN Media, economist says
item.description: In an ASX announcement on Wednesday, ARN Media agreed to pay the shock jock $12.09 million, with $3 million payable as soon as next month.

item.title: Perry leads Australia to T20 World Cup victory over Bangladesh
item.description: Ellyse Perry has starred with bat and ball as Australia gave their net run-rate a massive boost with a landslide win over Bangladesh at Headingley in the T20 World Cup.

item.title: Aged care tool under fire as man with late-stage MND loses appeal for higher funding
item.description: Melbourne man Graham Crossan relies on his wife Gaynor to help him eat, move and even breathe. But a new assessment tool means he has been refused funding for more support.

item.title: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia trounces Bangladesh at women's T20 World Cup
item.description: Ellyse Perry dominates with the ball as Australia waltzes home to victory over Bangladesh in the women's T20 World Cup.

item.title: Maroons Origin star Tino Fa'asuamaleaui reveals his dad's message to him before death
item.description: Tino Fa'asuamaleaui shares the advice his dad gave him before his death after a long battle with cancer.

item.title: How one tree in WA's Kimberley went from a place of pain to one of healing
item.description: In Western Australia's far north there is a "whipping tree" which for decades stood as a reminder of painful times, but has now become a place of healing and reconciliation.

item.title: After years of reform, a preventable disease eradicated in 'white Australia' is still on the rise
item.description: Years after losing his sister to a preventable disease, a grieving Gulf of Carpentaria man is demanding urgent action to address a disease found almost exclusively in remote, Indigenous communities.

item.title: Great Southern farmer combines love of farming and filmmaking to put her region in the spotlight
item.description: Farmer Brianna Jones has worked on some of Western Australia's biggest film productions, such as Blueback, Sweet As, and The Surfer.

item.title: Why Max Plath is the latest in a long line of Queensland State of Origin cult heroes
item.description: The players who fill the spaces in between the stars are the secret to Queensland's Origin dominance and in Max Plath the Maroons have found yet another player who knows just how to do it.

item.title: Why the power of Press Club addresses is in the clips posted on social media afterwards
item.description: When Pauline Hanson stood up at the National Press Club, it was not just for the audience watching on broadcast TV. In the age of social media, the bite-sized clips that come later are far more important.

item.title: Magpies admit Bobby Hill will not make AFL return for some time
item.description: Bobby Hill returns to Collingwood training, but coach Craig McRae says it will be some time before the 2023 Norm Smith medallist is ready to play in the AFL again.

item.title: Dockers down Cats as Bolton takes mark of the year contender
item.description: Fremantle comes from behind to beat Geelong for a 13th straight game, as Shai Bolton hits the heights at Perth Stadium with a Mark of the Year contender.

item.title: Crows legend Tony Modra seriously injured in truck crash near Victor Harbor
item.description: Crows legend Tony Modra has been seriously injured in a truck crash.

item.title: Are old buildings the solution to new homes in a housing crisis?
item.description: From former post offices to pumping stations, there are buildings with commercial histories all around Australia. Could repurposing them into homes help with the housing supply?

item.title: ABC news quiz: Soccer celebrations, bin battles, phone fails and an internet-famous duck
item.description: Today's news quiz is heating up like El Niño. Will you strut like an internet-famous duck or get schooled like Prince George?

item.title: Olive Eather's journey living with dementia on her family farm in central Queensland
item.description: After Olive Eather was diagnosed with dementia, the family farmstay changed its business model to keep her at home for longer.

item.title: Sacred Victorian Indigenous site Lal Lal Falls runs dry as upstream 'greed' blamed
item.description: Indigenous and environmental groups are accusing a Chinese company of hoarding water on a Victorian property and causing a sacred waterfall to run dry.

item.title: Australia's Rinky Hijikata stuns Jiri Lehecka at the Queen's Club Championships
item.description: Australia's Rinky Hijikata scores one of the wins of his career at the Queen's Club Championships.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands reveals new subscription-based show as his next move after $12m ARN Media settlement
item.description: Radio shock jock Kyle Sandilands reveals the details of his next move after accepting a "piddly little" settlement of $12 million in his contract dispute with ARN Media.

item.title: Report claims Coalition's renewable 'buffer zone' puts projects at risk
item.description: Energy experts have mixed views on what the Victorian Coalition's proposed renewable energy buffer zone policy could mean for the state's energy transition.

item.title: Wife of AFL legend Tony Modra thanks first responders following truck crash
item.description: The wife of AFL icon Tony Modra has expressed her gratitude to emergency services and hospital staff as the Crows legend remains in hospital in a "critical but stable condition" following a truck crash in South Australia.

item.title: Andy Burnham wins Makerfield by-election, poised to challenge Britain's PM Keir Starmer
item.description: Andy Burnham, a British Labour lifer, is expected to challenge Sir Keir Starmer for the country's top job after re-entering the House of Commons via a by-election.

item.title: Short on space? These are veggies you can grow indoors and on balconies
item.description: Even if you're short on outdoor space, there are ways to grow your own food at home. Here's what the experts recommend.

item.title: Voters surprised by failure to vote notices from SA Electoral Commission
item.description: Multiple voters have received notices from the Electoral Commission of South Australia informing them they will be fined for not voting in the state election, despite having voted.

item.title: Superbikes move to South Australia leaves Phillip Island community in limbo
item.description: The Phillip Island community is coming to terms with the loss of another major sporting event with South Australia claiming the rights to Australia's Superbike World Championship race from 2028.

item.title: Woman comes to AFL great Tony Modra's aid after Fleurieu Peninsula truck crash
item.description: Linda Cameron describes how she rendered first aid to the former Crows player and held his hand as they waited for an ambulance following a truck crash in South Australia's Fleurieu Peninsula.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Die Hard, terrifying tech and messy food
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Hanson's bulldozer moment could be a sign of new promiscuity in donor politics
item.description: One Nation's dramatic return to the national stage seems to have dashed any sense of restraint or shame about ties to wealthy donors.

item.title: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata lose in Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals
item.description: Alex de Minaur and Rinky Hijikata both go down in the Queen's Club Championships quarterfinals as they prepare for Wimbledon.

item.title: Indigenous language program aims to become Australia's first 'total immersion' school
item.description: After learning from Native American Yuchi families, Pertame elders and young mothers in Alice Springs are raising babies in a total immersion Language Nest and hope to extend the model into primary school.

item.title: Warning issued over counterfeit weight-loss drugs labelled as retatrutide
item.description: Megan Hancocks bought a drug labelled as the peptide compound retatrutide in cash from a beautician. Within a week of her second dose, she was in acute liver failure.

item.title: This warming apple and miso pudding will have you back for seconds - ABC News
item.description: Love a self-saucing pudding? This warm apple and miso version takes a classic to the next level

item.title: Ratugolea hospitalised as Port suffer injury carnage in loss to Magpies
item.description: Port Adelaide defender Esava Ratugolea faces a lengthy stint on the sideline after being stretchered off with a suspected ruptured patellar tendon during the Power's 27-point loss to Collingwood on Saturday night.

item.title: Country music boom breathes new life into Australian festivals
item.description: The country music genre has grown in popularity since the pandemic, spawning huge hits and an equally huge following in 2026 that nobody saw coming.

item.title: Adelaide Crows fans wish Tony Modra well as they take on Melbourne at Adelaide Oval
item.description: Adelaide Crows fans wish the AFL great well in his recovery after surgery as former teammate Mark Ricciuto says he might not be as good-looking as he once was.

item.title: Australia remains unbeaten in Women's ICC T20 Cricket World Cup
item.description: Beth Mooney has played down any concerns about her back injury after Australia crushed the Netherlands by 98 runs in their T20 Women's World Cup clash in Southampton.

item.title: Emma Louise on songwriting as salvation, and new album Sunshine For Happiness
item.description: It took seven years for the Jungle hit-maker and Flume collaborator to share the music that saved her following a "mental breakdown".

item.title: Did you pay attention at school? We'll find out with Guy Montgomery's Spelling Bee quiz
item.description: Think you can get 10 out of 10 on our spelling quiz? Just remember, autocorrect can't save you here.

item.title: A mass killing, bodies burnt and a 'conspiracy of silence': An outback massacre is not forgotten
item.description: Ronnie's great-grandfather collected bones and teeth from an outback creek bed. Now police say they're 'not proud' of what occurred.

item.title: Afghan Women's XI play for cricketing recognition in England
item.description: In a bid for recognition, the Afghanistan women's cricket team will travel to England for the T20 World Cup — an event they cannot play in.

item.title: Thunderbirds first team through to Super Netball Grand Final
item.description: The Adelaide Thunderbirds are through to their third Super Netball grand final in four years. Now they wait to see if they will face the Melbourne Vixens or West Coast Fever for a shot at the title.

item.title: Sharks antagonist raises Roosters' ire as Tricolours run through the finish
item.description: The Roosters finish with a wet sail to beat the Sharks 27-8 as Mark Nawaqanitawase gets a double in a heated but one-sided battle with opposite number Ronaldo Mulitalo.

item.title: Australia posts seven-wicket T20I win over Bangladesh as Spencer Johnson stars with ball
item.description: Spencer Johnson proves to be a menace with the ball for Australia, as the tourists complete a T20I series clean sweep against Bangladesh.

item.title: Ben Stokes returns to England captaincy for third Test against New Zealand
item.description: Captain Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson have returned to England's squad for the third Test against New Zealand at Trent Bridge following the conclusion of a disciplinary hearing.

item.title: ABC Classic announces 12 semifinalists for the 2026 ABC Young Performers Awards
item.description: Twelve young musicians, including pianists, string players, saxophonists and more, will compete in the live performance round for the ABC Young Performers Awards in August.

item.title: Final Alice Springs Beanie Festival brings joy to nearly sellout crowds
item.description: The 30th and final Alice Springs Beanie Festival has been a sellout, setting a new record of $400,000 worth of beanies sold in three days.

item.title: Taylor Adams announces AFL retirement after 15 years with Swans, Giants and Magpies
item.description: Hard-luck Sydney midfielder Taylor Adams has called time on his AFL career after a tough run with soft-tissue injuries.

item.title: Newcastle Knights player facing judiciary after alleged homophobic slur in NSW Cup match
item.description: Asu Kepaoa will face the NSW Rugby League judiciary after being sent off in a NSW Cup game for allegedly using a homophobic slur.

item.title: Runaway Tasmanian devil 'Mary' discharged from hospital
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of United Kingdom
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer says he will resign as prime minister of the United Kingdom and spend more time as a husband and father.

item.title: AFL legend Tony Modra's family 'extremely grateful' for support after crash
item.description: The family of AFL icon Tony Modra has released a statement thanking the community for the "overwhelming show of love" they have received since he was seriously injured in a crash last week.

item.title: Not even a landslide victory was enough to save Sir Keir Starmer's prime ministership
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer, the former prosecutor who styled himself as a safe, even boring, pair of hands became the first Labour PM in 14 years to win in a landslide. It was not enough to keep him in the job.

item.title: Deputy coroner releases findings into death of teen who took his own life
item.description: A 17-year-old school captain, who was dux of year 12 and a talented athlete, appeared to have everything ahead of him. Then he took his own life.

item.title: Australian Labor learnt from leadership chaos. Britain could be about to
item.description: After the era of Rudd-Gillard-Rudd, the ALP said: "never again." Sir Keir Starmer's resignation on Monday means we're about to find out if Britain's Labour MPs were watching.

item.title: This 'King of the North' could become the next UK PM. But who is Andy Burnham?
item.description: A popular mayor but a virtual unknown on the global stage, Andy Burnham is poised to become Sir Keir Starmer's successor. Here's what that could look like.

item.title: What's been achieved by the US–Iran talks in Switzerland so far
item.description: The US claims "great progress" has been made with Iran during the first substantial talks to take place under the new deal to pause the war, but there are already conflicting messages from Iran. Here's what we know about the results of the negotiations so far.

item.title: Ex-Wimbledon champion Markéta Vondroušová suspended for refusing doping test
item.description: Markéta Vondroušová, who lifted the Wimbledon trophy in 2023, is given a "significant ban" by the International Tennis Integrity Agency.

item.title: Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool to be drained again after algae appears and paint peels
item.description: The reflecting pool on Washington's National Mall will have to be drained again after algae and peeling paint appeared weeks after it was renovated.

item.title: How one letter gifted Cheryl her Indigenous identity — and two siblings
item.description: Cheryl van Wageningen was shocked to receive a letter revealing that not only was she Indigenous, but she had a brother and a sister.

item.title: Newly discovered ballista spider
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Lila McGuire celebrates success of First Nations talent in Logie nominations
item.description: With strong representation of Indigenous talent in this year's Logie Award nominations, actor Lila McGuire says it's a "very important time" and "a gift for young mob seeing so many of us excelling".

item.title: Union passes 'no confidence' vote against SA's police commissioner and deputy commissioner
item.description: South Australia's police union passes a vote of no confidence in the leadership of the police commissioner and his deputy, despite the top cop saying the move was "not reflective" of the feedback he received from officers.

item.title: Daryl Braithwaite steps back from live performances, saying the joy has gone
item.description: Australian vocalist, former Sherbet frontman and ARIA Hall of Fame inductee Daryl Braithwaite says he is stepping back from singing live.

item.title: Cosy ways to keep warm this winter without turning the heater on
item.description: Heating your whole house — or even a room — can be expensive in the depths of winter. Keeping the humans inside it warm is often cheaper and more efficient.

item.title: National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to collect data about what services work
item.description: Policymakers, mental health experts and those working on the front line will use the National Suicide Prevention Outcomes Map to gauge what services are working to prevent people from reaching critical crisis points.

item.title: Funding in NSW budget for 'world-class' robotic surgery in Orange
item.description: A long-running campaign to secure robotic surgery in western NSW has succeeded with the NSW budget allocating almost $10 million for equipment.

item.title: Siri Hustvedt reflects on the death of her husband, Paul Auster, in her new memoir, Ghost Stories
item.description: The author of What I Loved has published a moving memoir drawing on letters and journals documenting the last days of Paul Auster, her partner in life and writing.

item.title: Illegal e-bikes crushed after Tweed-Byron police crackdown
item.description: Police on the NSW Far North Coast have confiscated and crushed 35 e-bikes, and warn even more could soon be trashed as the crackdown continues.

item.title: Qantas plane to be inspected urgently after cracks found in Airbus A380 aircraft
item.description: Airbus says it will inspect 16 A380 planes, including one Qantas plane, after cracks were found in a key wing component.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's interview with Tommy Robinson pulled from social media and podcast platforms
item.description: Today show host-turned-podcaster Karl Stefanovic has had his interview with far-right extremist Tommy Robinson taken down from social media and podcast platforms.

item.title: Limestone Coast farming couple Brian and Val Collins celebrate 70 years of love on the land
item.description: Farming couple Brian and Val Collins share their secrets to a long marriage as they celebrate their platinum wedding anniversary.

item.title: Second Mongols bikie escapes from custody in the NT within month
item.description: A "known associate" of the Mongols outlaw bikie gang has been arrested after fleeing custody at Royal Darwin Hospital — the second time a Mongols member has fled custody in the Northern Territory this month.

item.title: Police to review interactions with Virginia Giuffre prior to her death in Western Australia
item.description: The interactions Jeffery Epstein accuser Virginia Giuffre had with Australian police before her death will be reviewed, WA's police commissioner confirms.

item.title: Two women charged after allegedly trafficking cocaine, meth to Tasmania on Spirit ferry
item.description: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

item.title: You may have heard of looksmaxxing but what about mummymaxxing?
item.description: Also known as mommymaxxing and mommaxxing — depending on which internet rabbit hole you're going down — mummymaxxing focuses on optimising motherhood and experts warn it could be harmful.

item.title: Pilot dies in light plane crash in Malabaine near Northam, north-east of Perth
item.description: Police say a light plane crashed in Malabaine on Wednesday morning, with the pilot and sole occupant of the aircraft found dead at the scene.

item.title: US Congress passes resolution to halt military action in Iran, but don't expect war to end
item.description: The US Senate approved a war powers resolution seeking to block military action against Iran on Tuesday. But don't expect the war to end.

item.title: Footage captures armed offenders at Townsville home, businesses
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Police believe Townsville gym shooting and suspicious shop fire are linked
item.description: Queensland police have released video of a gym shooting and a suspicious shop fire in Townsville in Feburary.

item.title: Neil the seal is back bigger than ever and that's a problem for his wellbeing
item.description: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land and a new video shows him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

item.title: Victorian Liberal MP claims assault by party colleague
item.description: The Victorian Liberal Party is in chaos after an MP claimed they were assaulted by a member of their own party room.

item.title: Can you guess these Australian movies from a single still?
item.description: Think you know your Mad Max from your Muriel's Wedding? Take our Australian film quiz to find out.

item.title: Nitrous oxide imports surge as Australian doctors report increasing harm
item.description: The importation of nitrous oxide has exploded in the last decade. Nangs are increasingly being used by some to chase a high and causing significant damage.

item.title: Road train fitted with CT scanner screens remote Northern Territory residents for lung cancer
item.description: A road train fitted with a battery-powered CT scanner is travelling across the NT outback to screen remote community residents for lung cancer, in the jurisdiction with Australia's highest diagnosis and mortality rates for the disease.

item.title: English cricket captain Ben Stokes speaks for the first time since nightclub incident
item.description: English cricket captain Ben Stokes speaks to media for the first time since he was banned from playing after an incident at a nightclub.

item.title: Decade-long fight for clean drinking water at remote Kimberley community
item.description: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their struggle to access safe drinking water amid a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap supply.

item.title: Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says
item.description: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

item.title: F1 Monaco Grand Prix result headed for court after McLaren, Red Bull appeal
item.description: As F1 descends on the scenic Styrian Mountains for this weekend's Austrian Grand Prix, two teams are in the process of reversing the result in Monaco.

item.title: Private security firm starts patrolling Ballarat streets in response to crime surge
item.description: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

item.title: 'Moist Mondays' help young Australians take the plunge and make new friends
item.description: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

item.title: First Nations artists turn ocean waste into sculptures on Cape York Peninsula
item.description: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

item.title: Public understanding of harmful algal blooms still low, survey finds
item.description: Most Australians remain uncertain about the cause of harmful algal blooms and are more worried about disasters such as bushfires and flooding.

item.title: AEMO expects more batteries and less transmission in the grid of tomorrow
item.description: Home batteries hit 600,000 as households play big role in energy transition, but building transmission projects remains "critical", according to the latest AEMO energy plan.

item.title: 'Respected' WA pilot Malcolm Vivian killed in light plane crash near Northam
item.description: A friend of 76-year-old pilot Malcolm Vivian remembers him as a "beautiful man" and "special aircraft builder" after his death in a light plane crash near Northam, and says he does not think pilot error was to blame.

item.title: Councils deny EnergyCo town water for New England REZ construction
item.description: Rural NSW councils already under water restrictions are drawing a line in the sand regarding supplying water to Renewable Energy Zone projects.

item.title: NT bikie boss sentenced to 13 years behind bars over meth and extortion charges
item.description: Secretly taped phone calls have captured the former president of the Mongols bikie gang's Northern Territory chapter discussing interstate drug deals while helping supply methamphetamine worth almost $800,000 to customers across the Top End.

item.title: SA council elections delayed until April 2027 after legislation passes parliament
item.description: The state government has passed legislation to delay South Australia's council elections until April 2027, after the Electoral Commission raised "very serious concerns" about its ability to administer the poll in November.

item.title: Former AFL umpire Michael Pell to stand trial over alleged Brownlow betting fraud
item.description: Ex-AFL umpire Michael Pell, accused of leaking information on confidential Brownlow votes, has been ordered to stand trial.

item.title: Farmers claim partial victory after Queensland government backflips on gas
item.description: Farmers are claiming a partial victory after the Queensland government backed down on controversial changes to gas mining approvals above a vital underground water source.

item.title: Illawarra man pleads guilty to offensive online comment about Anthony Albanese
item.description: A Wollongong man has pleaded guilty to using a carriage service to menace, harass, or cause offence after posting an online comment about Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese.

item.title: Qantas axes Alice Springs-Melbourne route, restricts flights between Darwin and Singapore to peak season
item.description: Qantas has axed its direct Alice Springs-Melbourne route after "many decades", and will also restrict flights between Darwin and Singapore to the peak tourist season.

item.title: ACT extends Indigenous Circle Sentencing in Supreme Court after successful pilot program
item.description: A pilot program allowing First Nations offenders to be sentenced by magistrates sitting alongside elders in the ACT Supreme Court secures funding for another three years.

item.title: Is the alliance between Trump and Netanyahu over?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has claimed hundreds of lives, health authorities say
item.description: At least 101 million Europeans were forecast to swelter in temperatures over 35 degrees on Thursday, with hundreds of people believed to have been killed by the heatwave.

item.title: ABC news quiz: Teals have a party, Neil the Seal does battle and Trump drains his swamp
item.description: This week's news quiz features the usual assortment of political animals and regular animals, as well as other odd happenings from across the world.

item.title: The 20 best albums of 2026 so far, from RAYE, Kacey Musgraves and more
item.description: If you haven't had the time to keep up with new music this year, we've got you covered. Find your new favourite album here.

item.title: Sexual misconduct findings not referred for Victorian Working with Children Check review
item.description: Victoria's former children's commissioner didn't refer sexual misconduct cases for a Working with Children Check review, despite saying she would never have done so in cases involving any sexual element "whatsoever".

item.title: Neil the seal not immune from new bird flu threat despite isolated lifestyle
item.description: Experts say it is a matter of when, not if, a deadly strain of bird flu reaches Tasmania, and popular marine visitor Neil the seal is not isolated from the threat, despite his relatively solo lifestyle.

item.title: Victoria's Alpine region police to carry long-arm rifles after Dezi Freeman officer murders
item.description: Chief Commissioner Mike Bush says Victoria Police officers in the state's alpine region will soon be permitted to carry long-arm rifles after the Dezi Freeman case.

item.title: Today show host Karl Stefanovic to leave Channel Nine, network confirms
item.description: Today show host Karl Stefanovic will leave Channel Nine after a controversial podcast interview with British far-right figure Tommy Robinson.

item.title: Rescue organisations say they need clear guidelines as vets turn away wild birds
item.description: Wildlife rescuers say they need help to prepare for a potential bird flu outbreak as vets issue statements saying they can no longer take in sick or injured wild birds.

item.title: What time do the Socceroos play today and how can you watch Australia vs Paraguay live?
item.description: The Socceroos can make it through to the knockout stage of the World Cup against Paraguay today. Here's everything you need to know.

item.title: Jetski riders rescue 'Princess Peach' the turtle stranded on Magnetic Island beach
item.description: A group of jetski riders were touring Magnetic Island when they stumbled across an injured turtle.

item.title: Abalone divers fuming as government slashes catch amounts by 41 per cent
item.description: The NSW government says a reset is needed to ensure the sustainable future of the abalone fishery along the south coast, but commercial divers say the reduction is a "pretty disgusting state of affairs".

item.title: Former Socceroos coach Frank Farina understands pressure of Paraguay clash
item.description: Former national team coach Frank Farina will be among the many Australians who will be tuning in to the Socceroos' decisive clash with Paraguay today.

item.title: Supergirl deserves better than a watered-down action movie
item.description: Australian Milly Alcock shines as Supergirl in the hero's first solo outing since 1984. If only the rest of the movie sparkled around her.

item.title: Police shoot and critically injure unarmed man charging at officers in Fairfield, Western Sydney
item.description: A man has undergone emergency surgery after he was shot by police in Western Sydney.

item.title: SA Police officers 'did not act appropriately' in handling of DV survivor's case
item.description: South Australia Police has admitted that three officers did not "act appropriately" in how they handled the case of domestic violence victim Stella Magnisalis, who was assaulted multiple times by her then police detective husband.

item.title: Karl Stefanovic's move to outsider politics follows a well-worn lucrative path
item.description: Listening to the culture war showman schtick he has so quickly adopted, Karl Stefanovic is now less a newsman than an ideological performer.

item.title: Why the live action Moana has come so soon after the 2024 sequel, original animated movie
item.description: Dwayne Johnson and Sydneysider Catherine Lagaʻaia have walked the blue carpet at a special event in Sydney. They told ABC News why now is the perfect time for a live action Moana.

item.title: Watchdog compels NSW Police to investigate after Sydney gay club drug raids
item.description: Sydney MP Alex Greenwich said the investigation call by the Enforcement Conduct Commission was a "welcome first step".

item.title: World Cup quick hits: Tony Popovic gives Socceroos fans what they want, Jordy Bos and Jackson Irvine star as Australia progresses
item.description: Tony Popovic gives Socceroos fans what they wanted, fan sites overflow as a "headache" epidemic sweeps schools, and Australia avoids the most nervous of waits. Here are the quick hits from the Socceroos' final group game against Paraguay.

item.title: How Australians across the country watched the Socceroos and Paraguay World Cup match
item.description: Crowds across the nation adorned in green and gold gathered in support of the Socceroos for their third match in the FIFA World Cup.

item.title: Detectives probe man's death in police presence and woman's death in crash near Meekatharra
item.description: Detectives are probing the deaths of a woman in a crash and a man who was found dead in a hotel a day later.

item.title: Small plane crashes into Beijing's tallest building, authorities seal off roads
item.description: Two bystanders have said an aircraft about the size of a car has crashed into Beijing's CITIC Tower.

item.title: Australia's next opponent locked in after World Cup chaos
item.description: The Socceroos will meet Egypt in the knockout stage of the World Cup after a stunning finish in Group G saw Iran have a last-gasp goal ruled out in Seattle and Belgium trounce New Zealand in Vancouver.

item.title: ABC 5050 quiz: Testing your knowledge of Bluey's friends, a glam rock classic and where to look for rainbows
item.description: Each question in the ABC 50/50 quiz has only two options: one is right; the other is wrong. But there's a catch.

item.title: Search for love takes singles off the apps and into the bush for old-school romance
item.description: Forget swiping right, singles are being urged to head to the bush in their search for real-life romance.

item.title: Government to shake up to home loan subsidy scheme for Defence force members and veterans
item.description: Critics say the scheme, which offers subsidised home loans, lumps Defence members and veterans with uncompetitive rates.

item.title: The Interview: John Williamson on what it means to be True Blue in 2026
item.description: Now that folk and country music legend John Williamson is 80, he wants to spend less time touring and more time at home eating his own tomatoes.

item.title: How on earth has the global economy survived this ongoing war?
item.description: An unfortunate side effect of the incompetent and erratic negotiating tactics of the Trump administration is that some of the underlying stories in the global economy can get overlooked.

item.title: Water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre 'best' locals have seen
item.description: The myth of an inland sea lured 19th century European explorers into the heart of Australia. But where they found only desert, this year it's awash, with water levels at Kati Thanda-Lake Eyre now the best some locals have ever seen.

item.title: Beer follows wine industry's lead with terroir-driven ales
item.description: A boutique South Australian malt house is producing flavour-focused malt from barley grown in different regions.

item.title: H5N1 bird flu a deadly threat to native duck species already menaced from all angles
item.description: Australia's native duck species were already threatened by urban sprawl, climate change, hunting and habitat degradation. Now H5N1 bird flu has been discovered on our shores.

item.title: Tony Popovic has made some surprising selection calls at the World Cup, but he's not going to discuss his reasons
item.description: When the Socceroos team to face Paraguay was named, there were a record six changes from the side that lost to the USA. But Tony Popovic won't explain why.

item.title: Hikers lost in Kosciuszko National Park rescued by drones powered by AI
item.description: Rescuers were able to locate the hikers less than five hours after they were reported missing.

item.title: Can the Victorian Liberal Party avoid falling apart again?
item.description: The Victorian Liberal Party is competitive in the polls just months out from the November state election. But will party infighting blunt its winning edge?

item.title: Queensland conservationists brace for potential bird flu battle
item.description: Home to the highest number of bird species in Australia, the sunshine state could have the most to lose from a bird flu outbreak.

item.title: Experts warn the polyphagous shot-hole borer could wreak havoc on Brisbane's parks if it reaches the east coast
item.description: A sesame seed-sized pest has destroyed 4,800 trees in Perth. Experts warn Brisbane's hot climate and dense tree population could be the perfect breeding ground for the pest.

item.title: NSW budget another 'missed opportunity' for First Nations communities
item.description: There have been three state budgets since the failed Voice referendum in October 2023, and this year marked the fourth for the Minns Labor government.

item.title: Virginia Trioli's final column: 27 things I've learned at the ABC
item.description: After almost 40 years in Australian journalism, 27 of them at the ABC, I am now in possession of enough mistakes to claim a small amount of experience.

item.title: Dung beetle exchange between South Australia and Tasmania ensures manure is managed all year round
item.description: While some dung beetles rest, others get to work, and farmers are making trades.

item.title: Rainbow flags wave in Seattle after Egypt and Iran pushed back against World Cup 'Pride Match'
item.description: Rainbow flags wave around Seattle after Egypt and Iran asked FIFA to keep a Pride celebration out of its World Cup match.

item.title: Butters coy about Power future after starring in upset Showdown win
item.description: Following a masterful display, Zak Butters is awarded the Showdown Medal in what may be his last match for the Power against the Crows.

item.title: Cabo Verde's run to World Cup knockout stage has them believing in impossible
item.description: Cabo Verde has secured the most unlikely spot in the World Cup knockouts, and the nation of 500,000 people is daring to dream.

item.title: Pete Buttigieg victim of false anonymous report alleging he was a 'danger' to his children
item.description: High profile Democrat Pete Buttigieg has been the victim of a false anonymous report, alleging he was a 'danger' to his four-year-old twins.

item.title: Crime scene declared after man shot by Queensland police north of Beaudesert
item.description: A man has been shot by police south of Brisbane after he allegedly confronted officers while carrying a rifle.

item.title: Cold front damages roofs, downs trees in Perth and WA's South West, with more rain to come
item.description: Perth residents are bracing for more wet weather after a powerful cold front damaged roofs and downed trees on Friday night.

item.title: Almost half of Canberra's local soccer match officials leaving the role after a year due to abuse
item.description: Canberra soccer match officials say referee abuse is a "systemic issue" across all sporting codes, with increasingly hostile behaviour from spectators, players and coaches causing 44 per cent of Capital Football's referees to leave their roles after a year.

item.title: Humpback whale entangled off NSW coast
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Vance vs Rubio: Which White House apprentice will win Trump's backing for 2028?
item.description: At times, Donald Trump appears to be harking back to his days hosting reality TV show The Apprentice as he leans into speculation about who he will anoint as his successor.

item.title: Best books out in June, including Whistler by Ann Patchett and Land by Maggie O'Farrell
item.description: June's best books include an Irish saga by Maggie O'Farrell, a gritty memoir by Ralph Jackman and a comic Italian romp by Andrew Sean Greer.

item.title: The truth about narcissists — and why most people get it wrong
item.description: As "narcissist" becomes the catch-all insult of the social media age, here is the crucial difference between everyday selfishness and a serious personality disorder.

item.title: Central Queensland family's 'heartbreaking' fight against motor neurone disease
item.description: A Queensland family shares its journey with motor neurone disease for the first time and gives advice on how to cope with a diagnosis.

item.title: The fastest human-powered Everest ascent from sea level
item.description: Avalanches, altitude sickness, orcs and Justin Bieber — a Brisbane mountaineer is believed to have set a new world record on Mount Everest for charity, despite every obstacle thrown his way.

item.title: FIFA World Cup history beckons for Socceroos against 'fearless' Egypt in round of 32
item.description: Egypt is sweating on the fitness of star forward Mohamed Salah, but the Socceroos won't be complacent as they prepare for just the third FIFA World Cup knockout encounter in the team's history.

item.title: Key remote bird observatory shut for months before Australia's first bird flu detection
item.description: Volunteers and wildlife advocates push to reopen the Eyre Bird Observatory, arguing the station will be critical in monitoring the potential impact of avian flu.

item.title: Griffith's Private Michael Paice and the war medal he never talked about
item.description: Private Michael Paice never talked about his distinguished medal from World War II, but his story of extraordinary bravery is now being told.

item.title: Love story: Holding onto the glimmers of my dad, Blues legend Steve Mortimer
item.description: Erin Mortimer's dad, Bulldogs and Blues legend Steve Mortimer, was diagnosed with dementia at just 60 after years of concussions during his playing career. Erin is holding on to the special memories, and the glimmers of him that remain.

item.title: Hating UK PM Keir Starmer became a 'cultural phenomenon'. His friends are confused
item.description: It took only a month after coming to office for Keir Starmer's popularity to begin plummeting. Does it mean the UK has become ungovernable? Or is something else to blame?

item.title: Queensland Police counterterrorism training operation at Lang Park
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland Police counterterrorism training at Indooroopilly shopping centre
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Queensland police conduct counterterrorism training at shopping centres, stadiums
item.description: A scenario based on the deadly Bondi Junction shopping centre stabbings in 2024 has been used to test officers as part of specialist counterterrorism training.

item.title: Man charged after four-month-old baby's death in 2024
item.description: The 22-year-old Bundaberg man has been charged with domestic violence offences of murder and torture.

item.title: Young station ringers win Battle of the Barkly at the NT outback's Brunette Races
item.description: Based on the day in the life of an outback station ringer, the Battle of the Barkly obstacle course has been won by two young cattle industry workers.

item.title: Blues ring in the changes as To'o dumped for decider
item.description: New South Wales coach Laurie Daley has made five changes to the Blues side for Origin III with Brian To'o missing out for the first time in over five years.

item.title: Jackson the star as Dockers make it 14 wins in a row
item.description: The Fremantle Dockers keep rolling along, winning their 14th straight game with a dominant victory over Gold Coast after the Kangaroos beat Essendon. Follow live.

item.title: Sydney and Melbourne record worst property auction clearance results in years
item.description: Sydney and Melbourne record their weakest auction results in years, with fewer than half of all homes going under the hammer finding a buyer.

item.title: World Cup: How the final qualification place was decided by 90 minutes of chaos between Algeria and Austria
item.description: Two stoppage-time goals, ghosts of a World Cup scandal from 42 years ago, and a desperately unlucky Iranian side are just some of the takeaways from Algeria and Austria's chaotic group stage finale.

item.title: Richard Scolyer's state memorial service to be held at Sydney Opera House on July 13
item.description: The former Australian of the Year and leading melanoma pathologist died on June 7 at the age of 59, three years after being diagnosed with an incurable brain cancer.

item.title: Live: Russell holds off Verstappen to win in Austria, Piastri claims fourth
item.description: Mercedes driver George Russell has taken his first victory since the season-opener in Melbourne, holding off Red Bull's Max Verstappen to win the Austrian Grand Prix. Follow live.

item.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing all 11 people on board
item.description: A small aircraft carrying skydivers has crashed in eastern France, killing all 11 people on board, local authorities say.
